data.title: Consumers Energy declares quarterly dividend on preferred stock, payable on April 1, 2026, to shareholders of record. (Characters: 103)
data.description: In Jackson, Michigan, on February 13, 2026, the Board of Directors of Consumers Energy, a key subsidiary of CMS Energy, announced a quarterly dividend for its preferred stock. Shareholders of record at the end of the day will receive this dividend on April 1, 2026.

data.title: Patricia Dlamini celebrates Black voices in romance novel writing, shares writing advice for authenticity. DURBAN, South Africa, Feb. 13, 2026 /PRNewswire.
data.description: In honor of her celebration, Patricia Dlamini discusses the most valuable writing tip she ever got, urging writers to incorporate authenticity, courage, and cultural veracity in their writing. The event took place in Durban, South Africa on February 13, 2026. This message was shared during the commemorations for February's Black History Month celebrations.

data.title: BTC hits $68,900 as US CPI slows; Fed unlikely to cut rates. Bulls hopeful for "higher low" in BTC.
data.description: Following a subdued US CPI inflation data, Bitcoin bulls experienced a surge, pushing the cryptocurrency to $68,900. This rally was fueled by optimism that the price of BTC would establish a "higher low."

data.title: Bitcoin has dropped 50% with reduced volatility. VanEck's Matthew Sigel warns no bailout for Bitcoin amid regulatory uncertainty.
data.description: Bitcoin has experienced a 50% decline from its peak, while volatility has significantly decreased compared to previous cycles, as stated by Matthew Sigel, the head of digital assets research at VanEck. In an interview on CNBC's Market Alert, Sigel noted that the current correction is substantial but differs structurally from past downturns. He emphasized that Bitcoin lacks a CEO and is not eligible for a bailout, highlighting regulatory uncertainties such as delays related to the Clarity Act as potential factors contributing to selling pressure. Additionally, market anxiety heightened after reports emerged of the Trump family selling a portion of their holdings in World.

data.title: Figure Technology Solutions, Inc. (Nasdaq: FIGR) launches secondary offering of up to 4,230,000 shares of Series A Blockchain Common Stock, aiming for $30 million repurchase.
data.description: In New York on February 13, 2026, Figure Technology Solutions, Inc. (Nasdaq: FIGR) announced the launch of a secondary public offering for up to 4,230,000 shares of its Series A Blockchain Common Stock. If the offering is completed, Figure plans to buy back shares of its Class A common stock from underwriters, totaling $30 million. The repurchase price per share will match the price paid by the underwriters to the selling stockholders. Figure will use its available cash to fund this share repurchase.
Supreme Court: February 20th to be the first opinion day amid tariff wait. Also sets February 24th and 25th as days for opinions.

data.title: Duke Energy helps Florida customers post-cold snap: Expect higher power bills, but assistance available.
data.description: Residents in St. Petersburg, Florida can expect an increase in power bills following a recent cold snap. Duke Energy is offering assistance to customers by providing resources for bill payments and essential information to prevent fraud. The rise in bills comes after the cold snap and is expected to impact households significantly. Customers are advised to stay informed about the potential increase in their power bills and to take advantage of the support provided by Duke Energy to manage payments effectively and safeguard against fraudulent activities.

data.title: FedEx joins Hedera Council along with Google, IBM, Dell to run a network node for supply chain transformation.
data.description: FedEx has recently become a member of the Hedera Council, joining other notable companies such as Google, IBM, and Dell. As part of this council, FedEx will operate a network node and will play a role in the governance of the network. This move highlights the growing interest in blockchain technology, with Hedera Hashgraph gaining traction among major players in the industry. FedEx's involvement adds to the significance of the council, which now boasts a diverse membership with key players in the tech and logistics sectors.
Fear and Greed Index: 34/100 = Fear

data.title: PR Newswire and Stacker extend partnership to enhance earned media reach for brands. Feb. 13, 2026.
data.description: On February 13, 2026, PR Newswire and Stacker have officially disclosed a strategic collaboration to support brands in enhancing their outreach and effectiveness. As a top press release delivery service, PR Newswire will team up with Stacker, the premier article distribution platform designed for earned reach. This partnership aims to assist brands in increasing their visibility and influence in the industry.

data.title: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Michigan sells AF Group to Enstar Group backed by Sixth Street, for future success.
data.description: In Detroit on February 13, 2026, it was announced that Blue Cross Blue Shield of Michigan has finalized a deal to sell its wholly owned subsidiary, AF Group, to Enstar Group. Enstar Group is a prominent global insurance and reinsurance company supported by investment vehicles managed by affiliates of Sixth.

data.title: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP investigates claims for Beta Bionics investors. Investors with significant losses encouraged to contact.
data.description: James (Josh) Wilson, Securities Litigation Partner at Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP, is urging investors who experienced substantial losses in Beta Bionics to reach out to him to explore potential avenues for recourse. If your investments in Beta Bionics, whether in stock or options, resulted in significant losses, it is recommended that you contact Wilson directly to review your options and seek guidance on your next steps.

data.title: Lendlease REIT reports 1H FY2026 DPU increase to 1.85 cents, with 10.4% retail rental reversion. Gearing ratio lowered to 38.4%.
data.description: Lendlease Global Commercial Trust Management Pte. Ltd. announced its financial results for the first half of FY2026, highlighting the divestment of Jem office and acquisition of a 70% stake in PLQ Mall in Singapore. With these transactions, 90% of the portfolio value is now in Singapore, boosting exposure to suburban retail. Key financial highlights include a lower weighted average cost of debt at 2.90% and an increased interest coverage ratio of 1.8 times. Gearing ratio decreased to 38.4% while achieving positive retail rental reversion of 10.4% and 7.2% growth in tenant sales. The company secured lower energy tariffs, aiming at a 15% reduction in electricity expenses. Financially, gross revenue and net property income slightly declined YoY due to divestments, but distributable income grew by 11.7% YoY to S$48.6 million in 1H FY2026. The total units increased, with a gain from the Jem office divestment to be distributed in the future. An advance distribution of 1.33 cents per unit was paid, with the remaining DPU at 0.52 cents. The company is focused on delivering stable and sustainable growth in DPU.

data.title: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP investigates claims for Aquestive Therapeutics investors with significant losses. Contact to discuss options.
data.description: Investors who incurred substantial losses in Aquestive Therapeutics are encouraged by Securities Litigation Partner James Wilson at Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP to reach out directly to explore potential options. If you were impacted by significant losses in Aquestive Therapeutics through stock or options, you are advised to contact him for further discussion and guidance.

data.title: Investor alert: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP investigates claims for investors of Rezolute to contact about significant losses.
data.description: James (Josh) Wilson, a Securities Litigation Partner at Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP, is urging investors who have experienced substantial losses in Rezolute to reach out to him directly to explore their potential courses of action. If you have incurred notable losses in Rezolute shares or options and wish to address your legal options, Wilson is available for consultation.

data.title: CSG's rating upgraded to Baa3 by Moody's and affirmed at BBB- by Fitch, reflecting improved governance and financial strategy.
data.description: CSG, an industrial and technology group, is furthering trust in global financial markets. Moody's raised the rating of CSG's supported senior secured debt to Baa3 due to enhanced corporate governance post-IPO, streamlined capital structure, and a more cautious financial approach. Additionally, Fitch Ratings maintained CSG's rating at BBB- with a Stable Outlook, highlighting the company's stability and positive prospects for the future.
